condition.qualitative_description: SPI at or below -1.0 (moderate to severe meteorological drought) combined with VCI below 40 (vegetation under significant stress) confirms co-occurring meteorological and agricultural drought during the cropping season.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: SPI quantifies meteorological drought severity by standardising precipitation anomalies; VCI captures vegetation stress relative to historical NDVI range. Studies on kharif rice in India show that SPI and VCI are both positively correlated with productivity index, and their joint depression is a strong indicator of agricultural drought causing yield loss. In coastal alluvial plains receiving 900–1400 mm annually (AER-18), a single-season SPI below -1.0 represents a significant departure from the sub-humid norm and is sufficient to trigger crop water stress, particularly during sensitive growth stages.

condition.qualitative_description: Three or more kharif weeks under severe drought, or at least one severe week combined with four or more moderate drought weeks, indicates persistent water deficit likely to cause crop failure or significant yield reduction.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Persistent severe drought weeks during kharif directly reduce soil moisture availability below crop wilting thresholds. In sub-humid coastal plains, crops are typically rainfed or supplementally irrigated from shallow alluvial aquifers; prolonged drought weeks exhaust both rainfall and shallow groundwater recharge simultaneously. The combination of even one severe week with multiple moderate weeks indicates a drought episode spanning critical crop growth stages such as tillering or grain filling.

condition.qualitative_description: Four or more weeks in a 4-week moving window with less than 50% of historical rainfall, indicating a prolonged dry spell capable of disrupting critical crop growth stages.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Dry spells within an otherwise sub-humid season are a recognised cause of agricultural drought even when annual totals appear adequate. In coastal alluvial plains, soils have moderate water-holding capacity but shallow root zones; a 4-week dry spell can deplete plant-available water and cause irreversible stress during flowering or grain filling. This signal is particularly relevant for rabi and zaid seasons when post-monsoon rainfall is inherently low and irrigation dependence is high.

condition.qualitative_description: Seasonal rainfall below 75% of the long-term seasonal mean, or annual rainfall below 80% of the long-term annual mean, confirms a below-normal precipitation year consistent with meteorological drought.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: AER-18 receives 900–1400 mm annually under a sub-humid regime. A seasonal deficit of 25% or more represents a substantial departure from the norm and is consistent with India Drought Manual thresholds for declaring drought conditions. Annual deficits compound rabi and zaid water scarcity because groundwater recharge from the monsoon is insufficient to sustain post-monsoon irrigation demand from alluvial aquifers.

condition.qualitative_description: Monsoon onset delayed by more than two weeks relative to the historical normal onset date for the district, shortening the effective kharif season and increasing early-season crop water stress.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: A delayed monsoon onset compresses the kharif growing window, forcing farmers to either delay sowing (reducing yield potential) or sow into dry soil (risking germination failure). In coastal Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh, where kharif rice and pulses depend on timely monsoon arrival, a two-week delay can shift crop water demand peaks into drier intra-seasonal periods. Delayed onset also reduces total seasonal accumulation, amplifying the drought signal from sig_03 and sig_04.

overall_reasoning_note: Drought as a cause of water scarcity in coastal sub-humid alluvial plains should be confirmed by at least two primary signals: a depressed SPI/VCI composite (sig_01) and either persistent drought weeks (sig_02) or a prolonged dry spell (sig_03). Seasonal and annual precipitation deficits (sig_04) provide corroborating meteorological context. Delayed monsoon onset (sig_05) amplifies but does not independently confirm drought. In AER-18 coastal settings, annual rainfall is generally adequate in normal years; therefore, drought stress is most often episodic and intra-seasonal rather than chronic. Rabi and zaid water scarcity is structurally more severe because post-monsoon rainfall is low and alluvial aquifer recharge depends entirely on the preceding kharif monsoon. If sig_04 shows near-normal annual totals but sig_02 and sig_03 are triggered, suspect intra-seasonal dry spells rather than full meteorological drought. Always verify whether the reported water scarcity is confined to a specific season before finalising the diagnosis.

confounders.confounder: Groundwater depletion from over-extraction causing water scarcity that mimics drought symptoms
confounders.how_to_distinguish: Check annual_precipitation_mm and seasonal_precipitation_mm against historical baselines. If precipitation is near-normal but farmers still report water scarcity, groundwater over-extraction rather than drought is likely the primary driver. In coastal alluvial aquifers, saline intrusion from over-extraction can also reduce effective water availability independently of rainfall. Drought pathway is confirmed when precipitation deficits co-occur with the reported scarcity.

confounders.confounder: Infrastructure failure (canal system breakdown, pump failure) causing apparent drought-like water scarcity during dry spells
confounders.how_to_distinguish: If dry_spell_weeks and drought_weeks_moderate are elevated but SPI is only mildly negative and VCI is not severely depressed, infrastructure failure may be amplifying a mild meteorological signal into a severe on-farm water shortage. Ask farmers whether canal water or pump availability changed in the same season. Drought pathway requires confirmation from meteorological indices, not just farmer-reported scarcity.

sources.title: Drought assessment for kharif rice using standardized precipitation index (SPI) and vegetation condition index (VCI)
sources.relevance_note: Demonstrates that SPI and VCI are both positively correlated with kharif rice productivity index in Indian districts; supports use of combined SPI-VCI threshold in sig_01 and seasonal precipitation deficit in sig_04

sources.title: Management of crop water under drought: a review
sources.relevance_note: Reviews drought typology, dry spell impacts on crop water balance, and the role of phenology and monsoon timing in determining yield outcomes under water-limited environments; supports sig_03 and sig_05

sources.title: Water–food–energy nexus with changing agricultural scenarios in India during recent decades
sources.relevance_note: Documents that post-drought groundwater storage in alluvial settings fails to recover to pre-drought levels even after good monsoon years, supporting the link between seasonal precipitation deficits and sustained water scarcity in rabi and zaid seasons (sig_04)

recommended_solutions: Check dam repair using community labour ahead of monsoon
Emergency pre-monsoon water harvesting structure construction
